q: We will stay inside should it rain tomorrow.
a: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay inside.

q: Studying hard will ensure you pass the test.
a: If you study hard, you will pass the test.

q: Her coming to the party will make me happy.
a: If she comes to the party, it will make me happy.

q: Missing the bus means we will take a taxi.
a: If we miss the bus, we will take a taxi.

q: He can watch TV once he finishes his homework.
a: If he finishes his homework, he can watch TV.

q: In the event of rain tomorrow, we will stay home and watch movies.
a: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay home and watch movies.

q: Study hard and you will pass your exams.
a: If you study hard, you will pass your exams.

q: Should we finish work early, we will go out for dinner.
a: If we finish work early, we will go out for dinner.

q: Hurry or you will miss the bus.
a: If you don't hurry, you will miss the bus.

q: Getting the promotion will mean she buys a new car.
a: If she gets the promotion, she will buy a new car.

q: It rains. We won't go to the park.
a: If it rains, we won't go to the park.

q: She arrives late. We will miss the show.
a: If she arrives late, we will miss the show.

q: You don't bring your ID. You won't enter the building.
a: If you don't bring your ID, you won't enter the building.

q: We leave now. We'll catch the early train.
a: If we leave now, we'll catch the early train.

q: I feel better tomorrow. I'll go to school.
a: If I feel better tomorrow, I'll go to school.

q: Ko Lwin studies hard. He will pass the exam.
a: If Ko Lwin studies hard, he will pass the exam.

q: You don't listen to the teacher. You will make mistakes.
a: If you don't listen to the teacher, you will make mistakes.

q: She gets the job. She'll be very happy.
a: If she gets the job, she'll be very happy.

q: You take the wrong bus. You'll get lost.
a: If you take the wrong bus, you'll get lost.

q: They don't come early. They'll miss the meeting.
a: If they don't come early, they'll miss the meeting.

q: He helps us. We will finish faster.
a: If he helps us, we will finish faster.

q: You forget your lunch. You'll be hungry later.
a: If you forget your lunch, you'll be hungry later.

q: Daw Mya goes to the market. She'll buy some mangoes.
a: If Daw Mya goes to the market, she'll buy some mangoes.

q: They invite me. I'll join the party.
a: If they invite me, I'll join the party.

q: We don't hurry. We won't arrive on time.
a: If we don't hurry, we won't arrive on time.

q: She wears that dress. Everyone will admire her.
a: If she wears that dress, everyone will admire her.

q: You study more. You'll get better grades.
a: If you study more, you'll get better grades.

q: It doesn't stop raining. The roads will flood.
a: If it doesn't stop raining, the roads will flood.

q: They win the match. They'll celebrate tonight.
a: If they win the match, they'll celebrate tonight.

q: You don't close the window. The room will get cold.
a: If you don't close the window, the room will get cold.
